questions.question: When exporting a page as a PDF on a Business or Enterprise plan, what happens if you select the 'Include subpages' option?

options.text: It downloads immediately as a single PDF file.
options.explanation: Incorrect. On Business or Enterprise plans, selecting 'Include subpages' generates a zip file containing nested PDFs and assets.

options.text: It creates a zip file containing nested PDFs and assets.
options.explanation: Correct. This option generates a zip file with all nested pages as PDFs, along with folders for images and other assets.

options.text: It only exports the current page and ignores subpages.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The option explicitly asks to include all pages nested inside the current page.

options.text: It converts the page into an HTML file instead.
options.explanation: Incorrect. HTML export is a separate format option; selecting 'Include subpages' for PDF affects the PDF generation process.

questions.question: Which of the following is included when exporting a page as HTML?

options.text: Only the text content without any formatting.
options.explanation: Incorrect. HTML exports preserve formatting and include comments.

options.text: Page and block level comments, including resolved ones and mentioned users/files.
options.explanation: Correct. HTML exports include comments at both page and block levels, covering resolved, unresolved, and mentions.

options.text: Only the images, with text stripped out.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Both text and images are included in the export.

options.text: Subpages are always exported as separate folders automatically.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Subpages must be explicitly enabled via the 'Include subpages' option.

questions.question: How are Callout blocks handled when exporting a page as Markdown?

options.text: They are exported as standard Markdown syntax.
options.explanation: Incorrect. There is no Markdown equivalent for Callout blocks, so they are exported as HTML.

options.text: They are exported as HTML.
options.explanation: Correct. Since Markdown does not have a native callout syntax, Notion exports these blocks as HTML.

options.text: They are converted to plain text.
options.explanation: Incorrect. They are not converted to plain text; they become HTML code.

options.text: They are removed from the export entirely.
options.explanation: Incorrect. They are preserved, just in a different format (HTML).

questions.question: Can you print a 'Full page database' directly from the Notion desktop application?

options.text: Yes, simply click File > Print.
options.explanation: Incorrect. You cannot print directly from the desktop app. You must export the page to PDF first.

options.text: No, you must export to PDF first, then print the file.
options.explanation: Correct. The desktop app does not have a native print function for databases.

options.text: Yes, but only on the mobile app.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The mobile app uses a share menu, and the desktop app requires a PDF export.

options.text: No, the feature is currently disabled for all users.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The feature is not disabled; you just need to export to PDF as an intermediary step.

questions.question: Which format is generated when you export a 'Full page database'?

options.text: A single Markdown file.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Full page databases are exported as CSV files.

options.text: A single CSV file.
options.explanation: Correct. Full page databases are exported as CSV files, while non-database pages are exported as Markdown.

options.text: A zip file containing multiple PDFs.
options.explanation: Incorrect. That format is used for PDF exports with subpages enabled.

options.text: An HTML file.
options.explanation: Incorrect. HTML export is a separate option for pages and databases.

questions.question: What is the primary cause of Windows failing to open a Notion ZIP export?

options.text: The ZIP file is corrupted due to large file size.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The file is usually valid; the issue is with the file path length.

options.text: Windows path length limit (260 characters).
options.explanation: Correct. Windows has a default limit of 260 characters for file paths, which can be exceeded by nested folder structures.

options.text: Insufficient RAM to extract the files.
options.explanation: Incorrect. RAM is rarely the issue with ZIP extraction.

options.text: The ZIP file is encrypted and requires a password.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Notion ZIP exports are not password protected.

questions.question: How long does the download link for exporting an entire workspace remain valid?

options.text: It never expires.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The link expires after 7 days.

options.text: 7 days.
options.explanation: Correct. The email link provided for workspace exports expires after 7 days.

options.text: 30 days.
options.explanation: Incorrect. While exports can take up to 30 hours to process, the link itself is only valid for 7 days.

options.text: 24 hours.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The link is valid for 7 days, not 24 hours.

questions.question: What level of access is required for a guest user to see the export option?

options.text: Full access to the page or database.
options.explanation: Correct. Guests must have 'Full access' to a specific page or database to utilize the export feature.

options.text: Can edit permission on the workspace.
options.explanation: Incorrect. 'Can edit' is not sufficient; 'Full access' to the specific item is required.

options.text: Can view permission on the workspace.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Standard 'Can view' permission does not grant access to the export menu.

options.text: No access is required.
options.explanation: Incorrect. Export options are restricted to users with sufficient access to the content.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid format for exporting an entire workspace on a Business or Enterprise plan?

options.text: HTML
options.explanation: Incorrect. HTML is a valid format for workspace exports.

options.text: Markdown
options.explanation: Incorrect. Markdown is a valid format for workspace exports.

options.text: CSV
options.explanation: Incorrect. CSV is a valid format for workspace exports.

options.text: Excel
options.explanation: Correct. Excel is not listed as a supported format; CSV is the standard for database data.

questions.question: What happens to Custom Emojis when exporting a page as a PDF?

options.text: They are converted to standard text.
options.explanation: Incorrect. They are not converted to text; they are simply absent.

options.text: They do not appear in the PDF export.
options.explanation: Correct. The documentation notes that custom emojis will not appear in PDF exports.

options.text: They are replaced with boxes indicating missing fonts.
options.explanation: Incorrect. They are simply not rendered.

options.text: They are exported correctly alongside other emojis.
options.explanation: Incorrect. This is a specific limitation of the PDF export format.

questions.question: How can you fix a Windows issue preventing the opening of a ZIP export due to long file paths?

options.text: Uncheck 'Create folders for subpages' during export.
options.explanation: Correct. Unchecking this option keeps files in a single folder, reducing the total path length.

options.text: Use a different browser like Chrome.
options.explanation: Incorrect. This is a Windows OS limitation, not a browser issue.

options.text: Compress the file again before downloading.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The issue is path length, not file compression.

options.text: Reinstall Notion.
options.explanation: Incorrect. This is a file system issue, not an application bug.

questions.question: If a user's email notifications are turned off, how will they receive the export link?

options.text: They will not receive a link at all.
options.explanation: Incorrect. They will receive a link, but it will be in their in-app inbox, not their email.

options.text: They will receive the link via email.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The article specifies that email notifications must be on for email delivery.

options.text: They will receive the link in their in-app inbox.
options.explanation: Correct. If email notifications are disabled, the link is delivered to the in-app inbox.

options.text: The export will fail.
options.explanation: Incorrect. The export process itself will succeed; only the delivery method changes.
